Motion For Protective Orders Regarding Evidence Code 1157 Privilege
Off Calendar Aug-16-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA's Motion For Protective Orders Regarding Evidence Code 1157 Privilege.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendant's New Patient Coordination Meeting may serve as a committee with the responsibility for the evaluation and improvement of the quality of care within the meaning of Evidence Code Section 1157 as testified to by Dr. Esserman. To that extent, the Coordination Meeting records and deliberations are statutorily exempt from discovery. However, Defendant's evidence also shows that the Coordination Meeting serves the function of treatment planning for individual patients such as Plaintiff. Thus, the anonymous notation ("refer to rad onc . . ") is a recommendation directed at future attendance or treatment of one individual patient. Further, the Coordination Meeting form itself contains checkboxes at the bottom for treatment recommendations pertaining to individual patients, including, with particular relevance here, the unchecked box: "Schedule new rad onc appt". None of Defendant's authorities holds that Section 1157 applies to future treatment recommendations for individual patients. Accordingly, the motion for a protective order is granted as to any entries on the Coordination Meeting form intended for the evaluation and improvement of the quality of care within the meaning of Evidence Code Section 1157 and denied as to entries intended for future treatment of Plaintiff such as the referral notation and the checkboxes for future appointment scheduling. This order is without prejudice to a motion in limine at trial.

Order signed.

Judge Pro Tem: James Fleming; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/JPT)
